inputs.prompt:
The shop is dark. It's been dark since Papa died.


But something is glowing above the counter.


A ghost. Cross-legged. Scrolling a phone. Like it's the most normal thing in the world — because for him, apparently, it is.


The son walks in. Keys drop. The universe holds.


Papa doesn't look up. He's busy. Swarg mein signal weak hai — he found a workaround.


One man frozen in the doorway. One ghost unbothered above the counter. The same shop. The same cash box. The same 26 years of everything that built this place — and one smartphone screen that's about to explain why it doesn't have to end here.

Animate forward from this exact frame. The old man ghost floats cross-legged above the counter — body gently bobbing up and down in a slow subtle hover cycle, 2–3 inches of vertical movement, completely weightless. His thumb scrolls the phone continuously, face expression unchanged — unbothered, focused on screen. Ghost glow pulses very softly with each hover rise. The young man at the door stands frozen — chest rising and falling in shallow terrified breathing, eyes wide and unblinking, one hand gripping the doorframe. He does not move. He cannot move. Moonlight holds behind him. 4 seconds. Single continuous shot.
inputs.mode: First and Last Frames to Video

inputs.prompt: Animate forward from this exact frame. Ghost Papa continues his gentle 2–3 inch hover cycle above the counter, scrolling phone, completely unbothered. The son — who was frozen at the doorframe — now slowly, cautiously takes one step into the shop, then another, body hunched low instinctively, eyes locked on the ghost the entire time. He moves like someone trying not to disturb something dangerous. His hands come up slightly — the universal human gesture of "I am not a threat please don't haunt me." Ghost does not look up. Does not acknowledge. Just scrolls. Moonlight follows the son inside as he moves. Single continuous shot. Camera locked.
